title: Poppyseed Cake
notes: This recipe was giving to me by my nurse preceptor during my senior year of nursing school at University of Arizona. I was assigned to a cardiac unit and had an amazing learning experience. This was the first location that I had ever found someone in full cardiac arrest and started CPR. Great news – the patient lived and I got this yummy poppyseed cake recipe.
In a medium size bowl, whisk together:
In a large bowl, mix together until well combined:
1 ½ tsp. butter flavoring or emulsion
1 ½ tsp almond flavoring (except if making for Grant)
After wet ingredients are blended well, add dry ingredients until fully mixed. Pour into 2 loaf pans or 1 tube pan, greased and floured.

Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Remove from oven and let cool for ~20-30 minutes. While cake is still very warm, poke holes on top with a fork. Drizzle glaze over cake. Let cool completely while glaze soaks into cake. (Keeps well frozen)
In a small saucepan, add the following ingredients:
1/3 c. orange juice concentrate (from the can)
½ tsp butter flavoring or emulsion
Heat until sugar melts thoroughly. Mix glaze until smooth and well combined. Pour or spoon over cake.
